title: SpaceX launching 45 satellites to orbit early May 3: Watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 45 satellites into orbit from California early Sunday morning (May 3), with the event available to watch live.

title: New for Star Wars Day, grab this Lego Star Wars model of The Mandalorian's coolest ship and get the Darksaber for free
summary: Lego released a new Star Wars UCS set featuring The Mandalorian's N-1 starfighter, with a free Darksaber model for a limited time.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 208 — Lander, Lander, Who's Got a Lander
summary: Episode 208 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the risks and realities of the new space race to the moon with Space.com's Mike Wall.

title: From new releases to classic models, feel the force this Star Wars Day with the best Lego sets from the saga
summary: Lego is launching new Star Wars-themed sets for Star Wars Day, alongside classic models. The company is highlighting its best sets from the franchise.

title: See May's full 'Flower Moon' bloom in gorgeous photos from around the world
summary: May's full 'Flower Moon' was captured in stunning photos worldwide. It will be followed by a rare full Blue Moon on May 31.

title: 3 planets will put on a sky show in May — when and how to see them
summary: Venus, Jupiter, and Mercury will align in the sky in May, offering a rare three-planet display. The event will be accompanied by stunning moon pairings, providing a spectacular skywatching opportunity.

title: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower peaks next week: Here's what you need to know
summary: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower will peak next week, offering a chance to see shooting stars and occasional fireballs when the constellation Aquarius is visible. This celestial event provides an opportunity for skywatchers to observe meteors under favorable conditions.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts get the star treatment after historic moon trip
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts received significant media attention after their historic moon mission announcement. Despite not having a parade, their mission was widely covered by major networks.

title: NASA Kennedy Center Director Announces Plans to Retire
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center Director Janet Petro announced her retirement. She began her career at NASA in 2007 and served as center director since then.

title: NASA Artemis II Crew Rings Nasdaq Closing Bell
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew rang the Nasdaq closing bell to celebrate the mission, highlighting international collaboration with CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen joining NASA's Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Reid Wiseman.

title: Key Support Equipment Arrives at Kennedy for Roman Space Telescope
summary: NASA received key support equipment, including HEPA wall modules, for the Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center's Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility. The equipment will aid in launch processing for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope.

title: NASA’s STORIE Mission to Tell Tale of Earth’s Ring Current
summary: NASA's STORIE mission aims to study Earth's ring current, a swarm of charged particles trapped by Earth's magnetic field that influences space weather. This research will help understand how Earth responds to changing space conditions.

title: Space Out This Summer with Variety of NASA STEM Activities
summary: NASA is offering a variety of STEM activities for summer, encouraging creativity, outdoor exploration, and future aspirations. The initiatives aim to engage the public in space-related learning and innovation.

title: Hubble Spots a Starry Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy NGC 3137, located 53 million light-years away in the constellation Antlia. This galaxy provides astronomers with a valuable opportunity to study the lifecycle of stars.

title: Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides
summary: Heavy rains from Tropical Cyclone Maila caused a deadly landslide in Papua New Guinea's East New Britain region. The disaster highlights the increasing impact of extreme weather events in the region.

title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
summary: Ireland is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington on May 4. The event will be hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and attended by Irish officials including the Ambassador to the U.S.

title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission celebrated its 25th launch anniversary on April 15, 2026, with a team reunion and the unveiling of a new global map of Mars created using THEMIS imagery.

title: What’s Up: May 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares May 2026 skywatching tips, including meteor showers before dawn, a Moon-Venus conjunction, and a rare blue moon at month's end.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its twelfth Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights with improved performance and safety measures.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday, marking a significant milestone for the country's space program. The debut flight lifted off from the Baikonur Cosmodrome, demonstrating the rocket's capabilities.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor, experienced in mass casualty events and overseas service, discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The mission highlights NASA's ongoing efforts in space exploration and medical research.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites in six launches across Florida, California, and French Guiana. This marks the return of Falcon Heavy and highlights the growing demand for satellite launches.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, offering fresh clues about ancient life on the Red Planet. This finding marks a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship Block 3 at the Cape, aiming for a launch this year. The first flight of Block 3 is approaching as preparations continue.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from the NASA Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report warns of potential setbacks in the development and deployment of the new spacesuits.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. The publication commemorates key milestones in space exploration and science.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test underwent a high-temperature 'bake-out' to ensure its reliability. This process helps remove contaminants and validate the parachute's performance under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly improve satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission passed critical space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes in-person and online sessions. The event will take place at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with preparatory online forums in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully transmitted from a strategic location. This milestone marks a significant advancement in navigation technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear industry executives are optimistic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which Administrator Isaacman views as a key step in the agency's evolution. NASA is significantly supporting nuclear propulsion and power initiatives.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion budget for NASA, rejecting the administration's FY27 budget request. Lawmakers have signaled their opposition to the proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space companies secured €1.4 billion in private funding in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in the sector's emerging startups.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to manufacture cancer drugs in orbit using microgravity to reformulate them for self-administration by patients. The company aims to revolutionize drug delivery by leveraging space-based manufacturing.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The product aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with detailed pre-construction and post-approval monitoring data.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and multi-domain military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance York's market presence.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The orbital data center market is seeing competition among major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, Sophia Space, Starcloud, Aetherflux, and Axiom, along with satellite manufacturing suppliers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit computing by running a large language model on a satellite to analyze an Earth observation image without transmitting data to the ground. The mission, Clustergate-2, launched on Momentus' Vigoride 7 space tug in March.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon stated that the Golden Dome satellite bus will use Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, implying no unique advantages over existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump over proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it a harmful move against science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation satellites for a French constellation in partnership with Magellium Artal Group. This collaboration marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite program.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: Orbital warfare systems have been prioritized by the White House's nominee to lead the U.S. Space Force. Lt. Gen. Schiess is set to succeed Gen. B. Chance Saltzman as Chief of Space Operations.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense, military, and remote area applications using Sateliot's satellite IoT technology.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions at Modern Day Marine. The conversation focused on how Lite Coms designs products to enhance network resiliency.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with France's CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. This collaboration aims to enhance frequency management for Skynopy's operations.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to enhance York's capabilities in satellite communications.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses longstanding concerns about interference and coordination in satellite communications.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as it transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming launch and its implications in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) announced plans to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This initiative aims to enhance technological capabilities in space-related operations.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to develop the WildFireSat constellation for wildfire monitoring. The government still plans to proceed with the space-based wildfire monitoring program.
